Scenario.overview: Water and Wastewater Systems Simple This scenario outlines a straightforward yet effective cyber attack targeting water and wastewater systems. Expert attackers employ phishing techniques to gain initial access, followed by executing malicious payloads, ultimately leading to data manipulation impacting critical operations.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Expert attackers send phishing emails impersonating water utility providers, tricking employees into clicking on malicious links.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 663; Name: Exploitation of Transient Instruction Execution
stage.description: Victims execute a downloaded malicious ZIP archive containing Pikabot malware upon clicking the phishing link.

stage.name: Impact
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 153; Name: Input Data Manipulation
stage.description: With system control, Pikabot manipulates operational data and commands, potentially disrupting water treatment processes.

Scenario.summary: The Pikabot distribution campaign in February 2024 demonstrates how expert-level attackers can compromise water and wastewater systems through a simple but effective chain of Initial Access via phishing, Execution of malicious payloads, and Impact through data manipulation.
